results.title: Awakenings
results.creator_credit: Penny Marshall, Lawrence Lasker, Walter Parkes and Columbia Pictures
results.brief_description: Robin Williams plays Dr Malcolm Sayer, a researcher recently appointed to a clinical position in a hospital for chronically ill patients. Amongst his patients are victims of a Sleeping Sickness Epidemic, unble to communicate with the outside world. Making a connection between this illness and Parkinson’s disease, he begins to experiment with a promising new drug. Robert De Niro plays Leonard Lowe, the first patient awakened by the drug, whose marked improvement prompts the hospital board to expand the benefits to other patients. As patients begin to engage in life again and confront the radical changes in themselves and their world, Dr Sayer deals with the emerging side effects. Based upon the book by neurologist Dr Oliver Sacks, who recounted his work with post-encephalitic patients afflicted with ‘Sleeping Sickness’ and his experimental treatment using L-Dopa. Cast also includes John Heard, Julie Kavner, Penelope Ann Miller and Max von Sydow. Screenplay by Steven Zaillian.

results.title: The Tree of wooden clogs = L'albero degli zoccoli
results.creator_credit: Ermanno Olmi, GPC, Ital-Noleggio Cinematografico and Radiotelevisione Italiana
results.brief_description: Scenes from the lives of three peasant families, living in the same farmhouse in Lombardy at the end of the nineteenth century, are chronicled over the course of a year. Their struggles to survive are slowly revealed in a mosaic of small, but detailed incidents which poignantly capture the repetitive drudgery and occasional joys of the exploited peasant class in rural Italy. Following the tradition of Italian neo-realist cinema, Olmi drew on a large ensemble cast of non-professional actors and shot the film on 16mm in a lyrical documentary style. The muted colour and straight-on, eye-level cinematography captures the striking beauty of the Italian countryside through the seasons without diminishing or romanticising the lives of the peasants who work it. In Italian with English subtitles.

results.title: Meet the parents [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Jay Roach, Jane Rosenthal, Nancy Tenenbaum, Robert De Niro, NBCUniversal and Universal Pictures
results.brief_description: Nurse Greg Focker (Ben Stiller) is prepared to propose to his girlfriend Pam (Teri Polo), but first must ask her father Jack (Robert De Niro) for his blessing. The perfect opportunity to do so occurs when Greg and Pam visit her family for her sisters wedding. Unfortunately, Jack is still trying to re-unite his daughter with her blue-blood ex-boyfriend Kevin (Owen Wilson) and Greg must prove himself a more worthy candidate for his daughter’s love. A series of unfortunate incidents and misunderstandings including flooding the backyard, setting fire to the house and losing the cat cause Greg to be rejected by Pam’s entire family, in particular her overprotective father, and Greg has to fight to gain their approval and convince them of his love for Pam. Also starring Blythe Danner, Jon Abrahams and James Rebhorn.

results.title: Out of Africa [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Sydney Pollack, Mirage Enterprises and Universal Pictures
results.brief_description: Meryl Streep stars as Karen Blixen whose love affair with Africa began when she and her husband Baron Von Blixen (Klaus-Maria Brandauer) went to Kenya in 1914. Despite her disastrous marriage and the failure of her coffee plantation, her passion for Africa never died. Robert Redford is cast as her lover, Denys Finch-Hatton. Based on the books of Isak Dinesen (Karen Blixen’s pen name) “Out of Africa”, “Shadows on the grass” and “Letters from Africa” by Isak Dinesen; “Isak Dinesen: the life of a storyteller” by Judith Thurman; “Silence will speak” by Errol Trzebinski. Her powers of observation and gift for storytelling revealed the empathy she felt for the people of Kenya and her attraction to the land. (Academy awards 1986: Best Picture, Best Director, Best Adapted Screenplay, Best Cinematography, Best Original Score (John Barry), Best Art Direction and Best Sound)

results.title: Kramer vs. Kramer [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Robert Benton, Stanley R. Jaffe and Columbia Pictures
results.brief_description: A sensitive drama revolving around the relationship between a single father and his young son. Dustin Hoffman plays Ted Kramer, a career-driver husband and father who returns home from work one evening to be told by his wife (Meryl Streep) that she is leaving him and their six year old son. Initially overwhelmed by the responsibility of juggling a career and the care of his son, he gradually adapts to the role of single parent. When his wife returns after a period of soul searching, Ted challenges her right to custody. Based on the novel by Avery Corman. Also stars Justin Henry and Jane Alexander. Cinematography by Nestor Almendros. (Academy Awards: Best picture, Best actor, Best supporting actress, Best cinematography, Best direction, Best screenplay)

results.title: It happened one night [DVD]
results.brief_description: Clark Gable and Claudette Colbert team up as mismatched lovers in this comedy classic. Spoiled Ellie Andrews escapes from her millionaire father who wants to stop her from marrying a worthless playboy. En route to New York, Ellie gets involved with an out-of-work newsman, Peter Warner. When their bus breaks down, the bickering couple set off on a madcap hitch-hiking expedition. Peter hopes to make a story out of their misadventures but complications set in when the runaway heiress and brash reporter fall in love. Screenplay by Robert Riskin based on the short story Samuel Hopkins Adams.

results.title: Gandhi [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Richard Attenborough, Columbia Pictures, Goldcrest Films, Indo-British Films Ltd, International Film Investors and National Film Development Corporation of India
results.brief_description: An epic account of Mahatma Gandhi’s lifelong struggle against British Imperialism in India, tracing his life from 1919 to his assassination in 1948. Filmed on a lavish scale mixing biography, history and adventure, Ben Kingsley’s gives an insightful performance as Gandhi. Co-starring Candice Bergen, Edward Fox, Sir John Gielgud, Martin Sheen, John Mills and Trevor Howard.

results.title: Bonnie and clyde [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Arthur Penn, Warren Beatty and Warner Bros Pictures
results.brief_description: Alternating between comedy and tragedy, this violent and kinetic re-telling of the story of Bonnie and Clyde is justifiably one of the most celebrated American films of the sixties. Set in the early thirties of the Depression, a car thief and his girl-friend commit a series of robberies which earn them a reputation as America’s most feared criminals. Featuring superb production values and brilliant editing, Arthur Penn’s film is an ironic exploration of the romantic roots of the counter-culture in the myths and memories of the Depression, and within Hollywood’s depictions of the “outlaw”. The film’s controversial use of violence is simultaneously brutal and liberating. Cast includes Warren Beatty, Faye Dunaway and Gene Hackman.

results.title: Coyote ugly [DVD]
results.creator_credit: David McNally, Chad Oman, Jerry Bruckheimer, Jerry Bruckheimer Films and Touchstone Pictures
results.brief_description: Innocent New York newcomer Violet Sandford (Piper Perabo) needs a job to support herself while waiting for her big break as a songwriter, and hears about a nightly well paying position as a barmaid in a popular bar. ‘Coyote Ugly’ is run by Lil, a fast talking determined woman trying to succeed in a male dominated world. Her staff of beautiful women including tough Rachel and sweet Cammie, nightly pour drinks and entertain the bars mainly male clientele. In a world unlike the one she left behind, Violet struggles to keep her head above water, by day peddling her songs to anyone willing to listen, by night dancing on a bar as a ‘Coyote’, and in between developing her relationship with Kevin (Australian Adam Garcia). Cast includes John Goodman, Melanie Lynskey and model Tyra Banks.

results.title: Nora
results.creator_credit: Pat Murphy, Bradley Adams, Damon Bryant, Trace Seaward, Gam Films, Road Movies Vierte Produktionen and Volta Films
results.brief_description: Based on the biography of Nora Barnacle, who remained James Joyce’s lifelong lover and the inspiration for much of his work. The film traces their sexually charged and turbulent relationship which began in Dublin in 1904 and survived through periods of poverty, jealousy, pride and betrayal. Moving to Europe to escape the confining traditions and religious attitudes of Ireland, the couple endured years of hardship and despair, interspersed with moments of extravagance, to pursue Joyce’s literary aspirations. The passionate relationship between these two strong-willed individuals survived until Joyce’s death in 1941. Ewan McGregor and Susan Lynch star as James Joyce and Nora Barnacle. Cast also includes Peter McDonald and Roberto Citran. Screenplay, Pat Murphy and Gerard Stembridge based upon the biography by Brenda Maddox. Music, Stanislas Syrewicz. Editing, Pia Di Giavla. Cinematography, Jean Francois Robin.
